ingredients.name: Chidorigafuchi Police Box
ingredients.localName: 麴町署 千鳥ケ淵警備派出所
ingredients.address: Chiyoda City, Tokyo 102-0082, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Tokyo police box near Chidorigafuchi appears in the film as part of the urban backdrop Suzume and Sota navigate during their chase through the capital. The distinctive box anchors a recognizable Tokyo streetscape fans spot in the film's city sequences.

ingredients.name: Shin-Kobe Station
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-3-1 Kanocho, Chuo Ward, Kobe, Hyogo, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shin-Kobe Station serves as a transit point when Suzume and the chair-bound Sota travel through Kobe. The bullet train terminal's distinctive location at the foot of the Rokko mountains is visible as the pair make their way through the Kansai leg of their journey.

ingredients.name: Oya Pond
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒791-2125 Ehime, Iyo District, Iyo, Kamimitani
ingredients.entityContext: Oya Pond in Ehime Prefecture provided reference imagery for the rural Kyushu landscapes seen in the film's early scenes. The tranquil reservoir's reflective surface echoes the abandoned resort backdrop where Suzume first encounters the mysterious door.

ingredients.name: Road Station Oya Kaigan
ingredients.type: サービスエリア / パーキング エリア
categories: サービスエリア / パーキング エリア
ingredients.address: 9 Motoyoshicho Mishima, Kesennuma, Miyagi 988-0273, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This roadside station along the Miyagi coast near Kesennuma reflects the tsunami-affected Tohoku scenery that inspired the film's emotional climax. The area, still bearing traces of the 2011 disaster, shaped the mood of the film's journey through devastated northeastern Japan.

ingredients.name: Kobe Otogi no Kuni
ingredients.address: フルーツフラワーパーク 内, Kamiozo-2150 Ozocho, Kita Ward, Kobe, Hyogo 651-1522, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The small amusement park Kobe Otogi no Kuni, located in the hills outside Kobe, provided visual reference for the nostalgic, slightly worn leisure facilities that appear in the Kobe chapter of the film. Its whimsical atmosphere matches the bittersweet tone of Suzume's stopover in the city.

ingredients.name: Higashiyama Shopping Street Square
ingredients.localName: 東山商店街 広場（休憩エリア）
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒652-0042 Hyogo, Kobe, Hyogo Ward, Higashiyamacho, 2 Chome−11−9
ingredients.entityContext: The covered shopping arcade in Kobe's Higashiyama district is referenced in the film during Suzume's brief stay in the city. The intimate neighborhood arcade captures the lived-in community feeling that Shinkai used to ground the Kobe chapter in everyday Japanese life.

ingredients.name: Kobe Seiai Church
ingredients.localName: 日本基督教団 神戸聖愛教会
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-1-27 Ikutacho, Chuo Ward, Kobe, Hyogo 651-0092, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kobe Seiai Church, a small Protestant church near Shin-Kobe Station, appears in the background of street scenes during the Kobe chapter of Suzume's journey. The historic stone-and-brick building lends the neighborhood its distinctive visual character fans recognize from the film.

ingredients.name: Suzume road
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-11-2 Furuishiba, Koto City, Tokyo 135-0045, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This scenic road in Hiroshima's Onomichi area shares its name with the protagonist and is identified by pilgrim fans as a location that visually matches several rural path sequences in the film. The winding rural lane is a popular stop for fans tracing Suzume's westward journey.

ingredients.name: Tokyo Station
ingredients.address: 1 Chome Marunouchi, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 100-0005, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tokyo Station appears as a major transit hub in the film, with Suzume and Sota passing through its iconic Marunouchi red-brick facade as they race toward the final door threatening the capital. The station is a visual landmark during the film's high-stakes Tokyo act.

ingredients.name: Wakinosawa Numata fishing port
ingredients.address: Wakinosawa-168-25 Yonesakicho, Rikuzentakata, Iwate 029-2206, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small fishing harbor in Iwate Prefecture near Miyako sits close to the film's pivotal Tohoku back-door site. The port's quiet coastal landscape represents the rural northeastern Japan Suzume's journey ultimately leads her back to, tying to the film's themes about the 2011 disaster.

ingredients.name: Near the Usuki High School
ingredients.localName: 大分県立臼杵高等学校付近
ingredients.address: 2521 Kaizoe, Usuki, Oita 875-0042, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The streets around Usuki High School in Oita served as a model for the rural Kyushu town setting of Suzume's hometown school. The stone-paved lanes and period architecture of Usuki are reflected in the film's depiction of Suzume's everyday life before she sets out on her journey.

ingredients.name: Ninomiya market
ingredients.address: 〒651-0094 Hyogo, Kobe, Chuo Ward, Kotonoocho, 3 Chome−3 二宮市場
ingredients.entityContext: Ninomiya market in Kobe's Chuo Ward provided reference for the lively neighborhood markets visible during the Kobe chapter of the film. Its compact stalls and everyday bustle capture the working-class city atmosphere Shinkai documented while researching the film.

ingredients.name: Apollo Station Saganoseki
ingredients.address: 2226-1 Saganoseki, Oita, 879-2201, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Apollo Station gas station at Saganoseki in Oita appears in the early stages of Suzume's road journey after she leaves her Kyushu hometown. The coastal road gas station captures the film's opening sense of an ordinary seaside town about to be upended.

ingredients.name: McDonald's Route 2 Suma
ingredients.localName: マクドナルド ２号線須磨店
ingredients.type: ファースト フード店
categories: ファースト フード店
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-1-6 Takatoricho, Suma Ward, Kobe, Hyogo 654-0039, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The McDonald's along Route 2 in Suma, Kobe, is spotted by pilgrim fans as matching a roadside scene during Suzume and Sota's travel through the Kansai coast. The mundane fast-food stop grounds the road-trip chapter in realistic everyday Japan.

ingredients.name: JF Oita Saganoseki Branch
ingredients.localName: JF おおいた 佐賀関支店
ingredients.address: 2016-4 Saganoseki, Oita, 879-2201, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The fishermen's cooperative building at Saganoseki in Oita features in the coastal scenery of Suzume's Kyushu hometown region. Its harbor-side presence is part of the working-waterfront atmosphere Shinkai used to build the film's opening setting.

ingredients.name: RAMEN CLUB Tokyo Station
ingredients.address: 内南通路 JR Tokyo Station, Marunouchi South Gate, 1 Chome-9-1 Marunouchi, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 100-0005, Japan

ingredients.name: Yawatahama Station
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-11-5 Edooka, Yawatahama, Ehime 796-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Yawatahama Station in Ehime is a key transit stop on Suzume's journey, as she and Sota take the ferry from Yawatahama Port to cross from Shikoku to Kyushu. The small regional station captures the rural, unhurried pace of the film's early travel montage.

ingredients.name: Juntendo University
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-1-1 Hongo, Bunkyo City, Tokyo 113-8421, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The area around Juntendo University in Bunkyo, Tokyo, forms part of the Ochanomizu district backdrop during the climactic Tokyo act of the film. Suzume races through this part of the capital as she tries to prevent the great worm from devastating the city.

ingredients.name: Near the Showa Daiichi High School
ingredients.localName: 昭和第一高等学校付近
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-2-15 Hongo, Bunkyo City, Tokyo 113-0033, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The street near Showa Daiichi High School in Hongo, Tokyo, is part of the Ochanomizu neighborhood that Suzume sprints through during the film's Tokyo climax. Pilgrimage fans trace this route to recreate Suzume's frantic dash toward the final door.

ingredients.name: Hijiri Bridge
ingredients.address: 4 Chome Kanda Surugadai, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0062, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Hijiri Bridge over the Kanda River in Ochanomizu is one of the most recognizable filming locations in Suzume, appearing during the dramatic scene where Suzume races to close the back-door threatening Tokyo. The bridge's iconic arch and the river gorge below are faithfully reproduced in the film.

ingredients.name: Ochanomizu Bridge
ingredients.address: 5, Bunkyo City, Tokyo 113-0034, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Ochanomizu Bridge, just upstream from Hijiri Bridge, appears alongside it in the film's climactic Tokyo sequence. The pair of bridges framing the Kanda River gorge form a widely photographed pilgrimage spot where fans recreate Suzume's desperate run to save the city.

ingredients.name: Yawatahama Port
ingredients.address: 1581番地26 Dejima, Yawatahama, Ehime 796-0089, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Yawatahama Port is where Suzume and Sota board the overnight ferry to Beppu, crossing from Ehime to Oita and marking the transition from the Shikoku chapter to the Kyushu finale of their journey. The ferry terminal is a documented pilgrimage stop tied to one of the film's most emotionally loaded travel sequences.

ingredients.name: Near the Ishidatami no eki TSURUYA
ingredients.localName: 石畳の驛 つるや付近
ingredients.address: 346 Yufuincho Yunohira, Yufu, Oita 879-5112, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The stone-paved lane in Yufuin, Oita near this traditional rest stop provided visual reference for the historic townscape scenes that appear during the Kyushu leg of Suzume's journey. Yufuin's well-preserved stone streets match the warm, nostalgic aesthetic of the film's southern Japan chapter.

ingredients.name: Anotobira
ingredients.address: 〒027-0202 Iwate, Miyako, Akamae, Dai 8 Chiwari 後ろ戸
ingredients.entityContext: Anotobira (literally 'that door') in Miyako, Iwate, is the real-world site that inspired the film's pivotal northern back-door location in Tohoku. The isolated standing doorframe amid coastal farmland is the closest real-world echo of the film's supernatural doors, and it sits in the tsunami-affected landscape that lies at the emotional heart of Suzume.

ingredients.name: Orikasa Station
ingredients.address: Dai 12 Chiwari Orikasa, Yamada, Shimohei District, Iwate 028-1361, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Orikasa Station on the Sanriku Railway in Iwate is one of the small rural stops Suzume passes through during her train journey into the devastated Tohoku region toward the film's climax. The modest coastal station with its single platform captures the quiet desolation of the northeastern coast.

ingredients.name: Sekigawa Station
ingredients.address: Doichō Kitano, Shikokuchuo, Ehime 799-0722, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Sekigawa Station in Ehime is identified by pilgrimage fans as matching scenes from Suzume's rail journey across Shikoku. The small, unmanned rural station with its surrounding farmland typifies the provincial Japan Shinkai documented for the film's Shikoku travel sequences.

ingredients.name: Yosan Line Hijigawa Bridge
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒795-0054 Ehime, Ozu, Nakamura, 424
ingredients.entityContext: The Yosan Line railway bridge over the Hiji River in Ehime appears in the film during the Shikoku travel montage. The graceful steel span crossing a wide river valley is a visually distinctive shot that pilgrimage guides consistently identify as a confirmed Suzume location.

ingredients.name: Shimokura Musical Instruments
ingredients.localName: 下倉楽器 お茶の水本店
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-２ Kanda Surugadai, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0062, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shimokura Musical Instruments on Ochanomizu's instrument-shop street appears in the background of scenes set in the Ochanomizu district during the Tokyo chapter. The street lined with music shops is part of the Ochanomizu streetscape Shinkai rendered in detail for the film's climactic act.

ingredients.name: Donomae Yonesakicho
ingredients.address: 519 Kushi, Minamiboso, Chiba 299-2216, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This rural hamlet in Rikuzentakata, Iwate is located in the tsunami-impacted coastal area of Tohoku that inspired the film's northeastern journey. The quiet community near the coast represents the human loss and lingering traces of the 2011 disaster that underpin the film's emotional core.

ingredients.name: Ochanomizu Station
ingredients.address: 2 Chome Kanda Surugadai, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0062, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Ochanomizu Station is the central hub of the Tokyo pilgrimage district featured in Suzume, with Suzume's sprint to close the back-door passing through the station's distinctive raised platforms over the Kanda River. The station's imposing elevated structure over the river gorge is one of the film's most recognizable Tokyo backdrops.

ingredients.name: Fujiya clothing store
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒189-0024 Tokyo, Higashimurayama, Fujimicho, 3 Chome−13−18
ingredients.entityContext: The Fujiya clothing store at Saganoseki in Oita is part of the small-town streetscape that provided reference for Suzume's Kyushu hometown. The modest local shop captures the lived-in, ordinary quality of the southern Japan community where Suzume grows up.

ingredients.name: Kiyosu Park
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-7-1 Kiyosu, Aichi 452-0942, Japan

ingredients.name: Bungo Mori Roundhouse
ingredients.address: 242-7 Iwamuro, Kusu, Kusu District, Oita 879-4405, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Bungo Mori Roundhouse, a rare intact prewar locomotive shed in Oita, provided visual reference for the atmospheric, time-worn railway infrastructure seen during Suzume's journey through rural Kyushu. The distinctive circular engine house is celebrated by fans as an evocative backdrop matching the film's nostalgic train travel sequences.
